mappings.evidence_note: Title page states 'GCSE Biology (8461) Specification, For teaching from September 2016 onwards, For exams in 2018 onwards, Version 1.0 21 April 2016'.
Linear qualification; students sit all exams at the end of the course.
Paper 1: Topics 1-4 (Cell biology; Organisation; Infection and response; Bioenergetics), written exam 1 hour 45 minutes, Foundation and Higher Tier, 100 marks, 50% of GCSE.
Paper 2: Topics 5-7 (Homeostasis and response; Inheritance, variation and evolution; Ecology), written exam 1 hour 45 minutes, Foundation and Higher Tier, 100 marks, 50% of GCSE.
AO1: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures. Overall 40%; per paper 37-43%.
AO2: Apply knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, enquiry, techniques and procedures. Overall 40%; per paper 37-43%.
AO3: Analyse information and ideas to interpret/evaluate, make judgements/draw conclusions, develop/improve experimental procedures. Overall 20%; per paper 17-23%.
Paper 1 raw mark 100 scaled x1 to 100; Paper 2 raw mark 100 scaled x1 to 100; total scaled mark 200.
Foundation Tier grades 1-5; Higher Tier grades 4-9 (allowed grade 3 if just below).
WS 1.1-1.6 Development of scientific thinking.
WS 2.1-2.7 Experimental skills and strategies.
WS 3.1-3.8 Analysis and evaluation.
Topic 4.1 Cell biology: cell structure, cell specialisation, differentiation, microscopy, culturing microorganisms, cell division, transport in cells.
Topic 4.2 Organisation: principles of organisation, animal tissues/organs/systems, plant tissues/organs/systems.

Topic 4.3 Infection and response: communicable diseases, viral/bacterial/fungal/protist diseases, human defence systems, vaccination, antibiotics/painkillers, drug development, monoclonal antibodies, plant disease.
Topic 4.4 Bioenergetics: photosynthesis, respiration, response to exercise, metabolism.
Topic 4.5 Homeostasis and response: homeostasis, nervous system, hormonal coordination.
Topic 4.6 Inheritance, variation and evolution: reproduction, variation and evolution, genetic modification, evidence for evolution, classification.
Topic 4.7 Ecology: adaptations, interdependence, organisation of an ecosystem, biodiversity, trophic levels, food production.
Topic 4.8 Key ideas: summary of key concepts relevant across biology.
10 required practical activities listed; Practicals 1,3,4,5,6,7,9 common with Combined Science; Practicals 2,8,10 Biology only.
Apparatus and techniques AT 1-8 (AT 8 Biology only).
Mathematical requirements for GCSE Biology.

draft_notes: Page numbers use printed/page numbers from the PDF footer.
Two-column layout: left column = assessable content; right column = Key opportunities for skills development.
Some right-column skill references may have merged with left-column content during text extraction; builder should verify.
